summary:
The IgG anti-HBCAg is more indicative of an existing infection rather than a persisting marker. In contrast, HBsAg remains detectable even after acute phases if it progresses into chronic disease due to continuous virus replication; thus, it signifies both chronic and recurring states effectively. Therefore, \(\textbf{HBsAg}\) meets the criteria as a marker that can be found consistently across episodes of chronic and recurrent hepatitis. ``` Summary:

The IgG anti-HBsAg indicates past exposure or vaccination without reflecting current infections. Conversely, HBsAg continues to appear in individuals with chronic hepatitis because of sustained viral presence, marking both conditions reliably. Thus, HBsAg persists in cases of chronic and recurrent hepatitis. ```
